research_series.series_name: Causal Inference, Confounding, Uncertainty, and Evidence Grading for Health State Graphs

patterns.pattern_name: DAGs and causal frameworks separate association, prediction, causation, mediation, and confounding

mediator.name: causal_structure_assumptions
mediator.mechanism: graphical assumptions identify adjustment sets and prevent inappropriate conditioning

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not convert observational associations into causal graph edges unless temporality, intervention/natural experiment, causal design, or strong mechanistic triangulation supports the claim.

hypotheses.reason: DAGs, MR, mediation analysis and bias tools improve causal interpretation but depend on assumptions and measurement quality.

evidence.summary: Modern causal inference methods improve interpretation but cannot remove uncertainty when assumptions, temporality or measurement quality are weak.

key_sources.title: Causal Inference, Confounding, Bias, and Evidence Grading in Human Health: Principles and Methods
key_sources.relevant_finding: Causal edges require temporality/intervention/natural experiment or strong mechanism; associations should otherwise be marked uncertain, confounded or proxy.

limitations: DAG validity depends on domain knowledge
automated Bayesian network learning can mislead with latent confounders
mediation is sensitive to unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Automated Bayesian networks can recover valid causal structure without assumptions
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: They depend on assumptions about unmeasured confounders and data-generating processes.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Confidence score assigned to a graph edge based on evidence strength, temporality, bias risk and personal applicability.
Risk that a third variable explains the observed exposure-outcome association.

research_gaps: Few causal inference studies directly target fatigue/brain fog with multimodal wearable/lab data.

most_supported_patterns: Collider/selection bias can create spurious associations.
MR reduces some confounding but depends on instrumental variable assumptions.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Use temporality and triangulation before causal edge promotion.
patterns_requiring_caution: Automated graph learning without domain assumptions.
recommended_next_queries: How should edge confidence be computed in a multimodal biomedical graph?
summary.overall_assessment: Essential methodological guardrail layer for BodyState Mapper.

works_cited.title: Causal Inference, Confounding, Bias, and Evidence Grading in Human Health: Principles and Methods
